input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a CollectionView with single selection and pull-to-refresh:

**XAML:**
```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel bindings needed:**

| Property/Command | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `Items` | `ObservableCollection<Item>` | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` | `Item` | Tracks selection (set to `null` after navigation to deselect) |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `IAsyncRelayCommand` | Triggers navigation |
| `IsRefreshing` | `bool` | Controls refresh spinner |
| `RefreshCommand` | `IAsyncRelayCommand` | Calls your API |

**ViewModel sketch (using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm):**
```csharp
[ObservableProperty] ObservableCollection<Item> items = new();
[ObservableProperty] Item? selectedItem;
[ObservableProperty] bool isRefreshing;

[RelayCommand]
async Task SelectionChanged()
{
    if (SelectedItem is null) return;
    await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
        new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });
    SelectedItem = null; // reset selection highlight
}

[RelayCommand]
async Task Refresh()
{
    var data = await _apiService.GetItemsAsync();
    Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
    IsRefreshing = false; // stops the spinner
}
```

**Key points:**
- `SelectionMode="Single"` enables tap-to-select.
- Setting `SelectedItem = null` after navigation clears the highlight so it's not stuck when the user returns.
- `IsRefreshing` **must** be set to `false` when the refresh completes, or the spinner stays visible indefinitely.
- Wrap in `RefreshView` (not the deprecated `IsPullToRefreshEnabled` on ListView).
